note: Start
Turn left at the junction

Bridge over the Váh river — check for pedestrians and cyclists, narrow crossing with limited visibility on the far side due to a bend in the road. The bridge surface can be slippery when wet, especially in autumn when fallen leaves accumulate. Approach with caution and be prepared to stop if necessary.

Continue straight, ignore side road on the right.                                                                                     TTThe road narrows slightly after the bend but widens again within 200 meters. Keep a steady speed and stay in your lane as oncoming traffic may appear without warning due to the curve blocking the view ahead of you.
Gas station on the left — last fuel stop before the mountain section, check oil and tires
Right
Narrow path near Lúčky, watch for pedestrians

Crossroads — proceed with caution. There is no signage at this intersection and visibility is poor from all directions. During morning hours local farm vehicles frequently cross here without stopping. Slow down to walking speed and check all four directions before you continue through. If in doubt wait for a clear view.
Follow the main road through the village, speed limit 30 km/h, school zone ahead
Left at roundabout, second exit toward Ďumbier
Uphill
Sharp bend — caution in wet conditions, gravel on the road surface after rain, reduce to 20 km/h
Checkpoint A
Straight

River crossing — water level may vary depending on recent rainfall. Always check depth before proceeding by walking the crossing first if unsure. The riverbed has loose rocks that shift regularly so the safe line changes from week to week. In spring after snowmelt this crossing may be impassable entirely.
Fork, keep left
Gravel road begins here, reduce speed, the section lasts approximately 2 km until the next paved road — watch for larger stones
Gate
Parking area on the right — good spot for a short break with a view of the Nízke Tatry valley
Downhill section
T-junction, turn right

Pass under the railway bridge and immediately turn left onto the dirt track. The entrance to the track is partially hidden by overgrown bushes on both sides. Look for two wooden posts marking the gap in the hedge approximately 30 meters after the bridge. If you reach the paved road going uphill you have gone too far and need to turn around.
Forest
Clearing with a memorial stone — stay on the marked trail, do not deviate, terrain is dangerous
Steep climb
Summit viewpoint — panoráma of the Tatra range visible on clear days
Begin descent, use low gear, the road is steep and winding for the next 3 km with limited visibility around corners
Village entry

Right at the church, then immediately left past the cemetery onto the main road heading south. The turn is tight and there is a stone wall on the inside of the corner so take it slowly. Trucks and larger vehicles should swing wide using the full lane width. After the cemetery the road opens up and you can resume normal speed.
Railway crossing — look both ways, no barriers
Continue along the river for 500m
Field road, possible mud after rain — 4x4 recommended or at least off-road tires
Small wooden bridge

Detour to lookout tower — short 200m side trip, view of Kriváň peak in good weather. The tower has recently been renovated and there are new information boards at the top describing the surrounding peaks and valleys. Worth the stop if you have ten minutes to spare and the sky is clear enough to see beyond the first ridge.
Rejoin main trail
Watch out, sheep herd often blocks the road in this section — be patient, do not honk
Cattle grid
Gravel ends, paved road resumes

Intersection with highway — wait for gap, no traffic light, visibility limited from the left due to overgrown hedges. During peak hours between 7 and 9 AM and again between 4 and 6 PM this intersection can be very busy with commuter traffic and it may take several minutes to find a safe gap to merge.
Gas station and refreshments near Ružomberok
Left turn, follow signs to Lúčky
Tunnel — turn on headlights, length 350m, one-way traffic controlled by semaphore
Exit tunnel, sharp right
Spa town — speed 30 km/h, heavy pedestrian and cyclist traffic, shared space zone with café terraces on both sides
Roundabout, third exit

12% incline — heavy vehicles use right lane. The gradient is sustained for approximately 1.5 km and there is no overtaking allowed on this stretch. Engine braking recommended on the way down. Several accidents have occurred here in winter when the road surface freezes overnight so check conditions before attempting this section between November and March.
Scenic overlook on the right, optional photo stop with a view of the Nízke Tatry mountain range stretching across the horizon
Descent into the valley

Last curve before the finish — slow down, blind section with oncoming traffic possible. Pass under the railway bridge and immediately turn left onto the dirt track. The entrance to the track is partially hidden by overgrown bushes on both sides. Look for two wooden posts marking the gap in the hedge approximately 30 meters after the bridge. If you reach the paved road going uphill you have gone too far and need to turn around.
Finish — parking at the Ďumbier mountain hotel
